title: JavaScript Boolean Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of booleans, truthy, and falsy values in JavaScript.

questions.question: Which of the following values is TRUTHY in JavaScript?
What is the result of Boolean(10 > 9)?
Which property can you use to get the type of a boolean literal (e.g., true)?
What happens when you declare a boolean as an object (new Boolean())?
options: It behaves exactly like a primitive boolean.
It is treated as an object and behaves inconsistently in strict comparisons (===).
It makes the boolean more efficient.
It allows you to add custom properties and methods, which is recommended.

questions.question: In a logical XOR operation (if available in JavaScript), how can you simulate it?
